journal_entry.description: Office relocation services provided to customer, payment due in 10 days
journal_entry.rationale: Atlas Van Lines provided services and has earned revenue. Since payment is due in 10 days, this is a credit sale. We debit Accounts Receivable (asset increase) to recognize the customer's obligation to pay. We credit Service Revenue (revenue increase) to recognize the earned income. The debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly indicates one asset increase line, and the credit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) correctly indicates one revenue increase line. No tax information was provided in the transaction, so no HST/GST lines are added (the stated amount of $31,500 is treated as the transaction amount as written).

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines provided services to a customer with payment due in 10 days. This creates an account receivable (asset increase). The debit side records: 1 asset increase (Accounts Receivable).

debit_classifier.reason: Atlas Van Lines provided services to a customer with payment due in 10 days. This creates an account receivable (asset increase). The debit side records: 1 asset increase (Accounts Receivable).

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines provided relocation services and will receive payment later. This is a revenue-earning transaction. The credit side records the service revenue earned (revenue increase). The debit side (not classified here) would record accounts receivable (asset increase).

credit_classifier.reason: Atlas Van Lines provided relocation services and will receive payment later. This is a revenue-earning transaction. The credit side records the service revenue earned (revenue increase). The debit side (not classified here) would record accounts receivable (asset increase).
